title: Dasa's Voyage
subTitle: Where logic is the only language, and technology the ultimate destination.
description: Exploring SBC, Linux, and the Blockchain.

P2.description:
## Changing software sources (mirrors) is a fundamental skill for any Linux user, especially when dealing with restricted or slow international bandwidth. Since different distributions use different package managers, the process varies. Here is a comprehensive guide to switching to Chinese mirrors (such as TUNA, Alibaba, or Huawei Cloud) for the most common Linux families.

## 1. Debian / Ubuntu Family
These distributions use the `apt` package manager. Configuration is primarily stored in `/etc/apt/sources.list`.

### **Step-by-Step Instructions**

1.  **Backup your current config:**
    ```bash
    sudo cp /etc/apt/sources.list /etc/apt/sources.list.bak
    ```

2.  **Edit the file:**
    ```bash
    sudo nano /etc/apt/sources.list
    ```

3.  **Replace the content:**
    Delete the existing lines and replace them with a mirror endpoint. For example, for **Ubuntu 22.04 (Jammy)**:
    ```text
    deb [https://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/ubuntu/](https://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/ubuntu/) jammy main restricted universe multiverse
    deb [https://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/ubuntu/](https://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/ubuntu/) jammy-updates main restricted universe multiverse
    deb [https://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/ubuntu/](https://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/ubuntu/) jammy-backports main restricted universe multiverse
    deb [https://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/ubuntu/](https://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/ubuntu/) jammy-security main restricted universe multiverse
    ```

4.  **Update the database:**
    ```bash
    sudo apt update
    ```

> **Note for Ubuntu 24.04+:** Modern versions use the DEB822 format located at `/etc/apt/sources.list.d/ubuntu.sources`. You should modify the `URIs` field within that specific file.

---

## 2. Arch Linux
Arch Linux uses `pacman`. Mirror priority is determined by the order of entries in `/etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist`.

### **Manual Configuration**

1.  **Edit the mirrorlist:**
    ```bash
    sudo nano /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist
    ```

2.  **Add the mirror to the top:**
    The first working mirror in the list is the one used. Add this at the very top:
    ```text
    Server = [https://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/archlinux/$repo/os/$arch](https://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/archlinux/$repo/os/$arch)
    ```

3.  **Sync system:**
    ```bash
    sudo pacman -Syu
    ```

---

## 3. Red Hat / Fedora / CentOS / AlmaLinux
These systems use `dnf` or `yum`. Configuration files are located in `/etc/yum.repos.d/`.

### **The 'Clean Slate' Method**

1.  **Backup and remove old repos:**
    ```bash
    sudo mkdir /etc/yum.repos.d/bak
    sudo mv /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo /etc/yum.repos.d/bak/
    ```

2.  **Download the new repo file (Example for CentOS Stream 9):**
    ```bash
    sudo curl -o /etc/yum.repos.d/centos.repo [https://mirrors.aliyun.com/repo/Centos-9.repo](https://mirrors.aliyun.com/repo/Centos-9.repo)
    ```

3.  **Refresh cache:**
    ```bash
    sudo dnf clean all
    sudo dnf makecache
    ```

---

## 4. Alpine Linux
Commonly used in Docker containers and lightweight SBC (Single Board Computer) environments like the Raspberry Pi.

1.  **Modify the repositories file:**
    ```bash
    sudo sed -i 's/dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/g' /etc/apk/repositories
    ```

2.  **Update:**
    ```bash
    apk update
    ```

---

## 5. SBC Specifics (Raspberry Pi)
If you are running Raspberry Pi OS, the mirror URL is different from standard Debian. Use:
`http://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/raspberrypi/`

---

## 6. Quick Reference: Top Chinese Mirrors

| Provider | URL | Best For |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Tsinghua (TUNA)** | `https://mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn` | Academic & General |
| **Alibaba Cloud** | `https://mirrors.aliyun.com` | Enterprise & Stability |
| **USTC** | `https://mirrors.ustc.edu.cn` | Speed & Reliability |

---

## Technical Analysis & Tips

### **1. Permissions**
Since you are modifying system files in `/etc`, you must use `sudo`. Without it, you will receive a 'Permission Denied' error when trying to save.

### **2. HTTPS Support**
Some minimal base images (especially Debian/Ubuntu 'slim' versions) don't support HTTPS out of the box. Install the necessary packages first:
```bash
sudo apt install apt-transport-https ca-certificates。

P4.htmlDir: docs/post/The Kernel in a World of GUI- A Logical Reframe of the INTP -Likability- Myth.html
P4.postTitle: The Kernel in a World of GUI: A Logical Reframe of the INTP "Likability" Myth
P4.description:
In the sprawling ecosystem of MBTI discourse—particularly across platforms like Bilibili or YouTube—the **INTP (The Logician)** often finds itself at the bottom of 'Likability Tier Lists.' We are frequently labeled as the 'socially detached,' the 'unintentional contrarians,' or the 'ghosters of the digital age.' As a 17-year-old navigating the high-pressure environment of high school and the complexities of human connection, these labels can trigger a genuine 'System Anxiety.'

However, if we strip away the superficial layers of social media metrics, we find that the perceived 'low likability' of the INTP is not a bug, but a feature of a highly specialized architecture.

---

### 1. The Command Line Interface (CLI) vs. Graphical User Interface (GUI)

Most social interactions are built on a **GUI (Graphical User Interface)**: they are designed to be intuitive, colorful, and emotionally reactive. They prioritize the 'User Experience' over the 'Source Code.' 

The INTP, by contrast, operates on a **CLI (Command Line Interface)**. To the casual observer, a blinking cursor on a black screen looks cold and intimidating. But to those who know the right commands, the CLI offers a level of depth, precision, and power that a GUI could never reach. We don't lack 'likability'; we simply require a higher 'access privilege.'

### 2. The Integrity of the 'Debug' Instinct

The common critique that INTPs are 'argumentative' is a fundamental misunderstanding of our core objective. We are not 'trolls' seeking conflict; we are **compilers** seeking **Internal Consistency**. 

When an INTP corrects a logical fallacy, they aren't attacking the person—they are identifying a **Syntax Error** in the collective understanding of the world. In a society that often prioritizes 'polite delusions' over 'harsh truths,' our commitment to accuracy is a rare form of intellectual honesty.

### 3. Energy Management and 'Back-End' Processing

What others call 'social withdrawal,' we know to be **Resource Allocation**. An INTP's brain is constantly running high-load background processes—analyzing systems, deconstructing theories, or simulating future outcomes. 

Maintaining a 'socially bubbly' facade requires massive amounts of CPU cycles. When we 'disappear,' we aren't being rude; we are preventing a **System Crash**. We prioritize the stability of our internal world over the vanity metrics of external approval.

### 4. The Intellectual Aesthetic and the Future

As we move further into an era defined by technology, blockchain, and complex systems, the INTP’s traits are becoming the most valuable 'assets' in the market. The ability to remain objective, to hyper-focus on technical nuances, and to see through emotional noise is a superpower.

---

### Conclusion: Trust Your Internal Build

To my fellow INTPs: Do not attempt to 'reformat' your personality to fit a popular social template. The world has plenty of 'Social Host' types; it has a desperate shortage of people who can actually think from first principles.

Your 'Likability' isn't a measure of your worth—it's just a metric of how many people are currently compatible with your OS. Focus on optimizing your **Kernel**, refining your logic, and finding the few 'Superusers' who appreciate the power of the Command Line.。
